data.title: U.S. government urges Supreme Court to dismiss Coinbase users' lawsuit against IRS for crypto trading records
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The US government has urged the Supreme Court not to accept Coinbase users' lawsuit against the IRS for obtaining their crypto trading records. Relevant documents show that US Deputy Attorney General D. John Sauer argued that Coinbase user James Harper did not have the right granted by the Fourth Amendment to protect his financial records held by the exchange.
It is reported that in 2016, the IRS launched an investigation into the widespread underreporting of taxes on cryptocurrency gains. At that time, the IRS found that millions of Coinbase users had huge tax gaps when trading Bitcoin, so it forced Coinbase to hand over the records of large customers. James Harper subsequently filed a lawsuit, claiming that the IRS's actions amounted to an unconstitutional search of his personal records. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: James Wynn: I'll be back, same as always
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: James Wynn, the giant whale, posted on the X platform: "I will make a comeback, as always, I will enjoy doing this, I also like games, I like swings, money depends entirely on the mentality, I made a big bet, carefully calculated, and made a lot of money, most of you dare not, you don't have such ability, and lack the mentality, my passive income every month is more than most of you make in a year. However, money can't make you truly rich, peace, happiness, family, love, loyalty, health, nature, sunshine, dogs, can make you feel rich. And I have kept all of this firmly in my heart. I am freer now than before."

data.description:

High fees, regulatory uncertainty, and allocation limits weaken its real weight compared to traditional index funds. Since 2022,. . .

data.title: U.S. government urges court to dismiss lawsuit over Coinbase users' cryptocurrency records
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. government argued in a Supreme Court filing that Coinbase user James Harper had no right to prevent the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from obtaining his cryptocurrency trading records. In a document filed on May 30, U.S. Attorney General D. John Sauer argued that Coinbase user James Harper had no Fourth Amendment right to protect his financial records held on the exchange. The government claimed that Harper "voluntarily" shared his data with Coinbase and that the IRS followed due process of law to obtain the data through a judicially approved subpoena.
Harper's case revolved around an IRS investigation in 2016 into the widespread underreporting of taxes on cryptocurrency gains. At the time, the IRS found that millions of Coinbase users traded Bitcoin, while relatively few taxpayers reported cryptocurrency gains, and subsequently forced Coinbase to hand over the records of its high-volume customers.
Harper, who traded Bitcoin on Coinbase in the relevant years, later filed a lawsuit claiming that the IRS's actions constituted an unconstitutional search of his personal records. A lower court disagreed, ruling that Coinbase's records were business documents, not Harper's personal documents, and that the IRS acted legally. In its complaint, the government argued that Supreme Court precedent supports the IRS's position. The government cited past cases that emphasized that individuals have no reasonable expectation of privacy in financial records held by a third party, Coinbase. The Supreme Court has not yet decided whether to hear the case. If it dismisses, the First Circuit's ruling in favor of the IRS will remain intact. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin and RWA financial technology company Matador receives $1.5 million in strategic investment
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Matador Technologies, a financial technology development company focusing on Bitcoin and tokenized real-world assets, announced that it has received a total investment of 1.5 million Canadian dollars from Arrington Capital.
Arrington Capital is a digital asset management company founded in 2017 by Michael Arrington, founder of TechCrunch and CrunchBase, Heather Harde, CEO of TechCrunch, and Geoffrey Arone. This investment will be used to purchase Bitcoin, gold acquisitions and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Stablecoin USDG now available on Ink blockchain
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Global Dollar Network announced that Global Dollar (USDG) is now available on the Ink blockchain. USDG is a single-currency stablecoin pegged to the US dollar issued by Paxos. It has been available on the Solana blockchain and maintains Ethereum compatibility. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Global Dollar Network Expands USDG Availability to Ink Blockchain
data.description: According to Odaily, Global Dollar Network has announced that its stablecoin, Global Dollar (USDG), is now available on the Ink blockchain. USDG, a single-currency stablecoin pegged to the U.S. dollar and issued by Paxos, was previously accessible on the Solana blockchain while maintaining compatibility with Ethereum.

data.title: GameStop CEO Views Bitcoin as Hedge Against Currency Depreciation
data.description: According to PANews, GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen considers Bitcoin a tool for hedging against currency depreciation. He stated that if Bitcoin becomes digital gold, its potential for growth could be even greater.

data.title: A certain address has accumulated a profit of $1.238 million through altcoin leverage in the past week
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News On-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa monitored that the address 0xe13…cf35c has accumulated a profit of $1.238 million through a basket of altcoin leverage in the past week. The strategy of this address seems to be "small position + multiple targets". Currently, a total of 36 tokens including ETH have been opened for leverage.
